summary: Technology executive and enterprise solutions architect who builds AI-augmented engineering organizations from the ground up. 20+ years progressing from hands-on cloud architecture and digital transformation delivery to leading multi-team engineering organizations—designing operating models, governance frameworks, and platform strategies that convert technical capability into measurable business outcomes. Career arc spans Fortune 500 consulting, regulated fintech platforms, enterprise SaaS modernization, and Healthcare IT at scale. Currently driving AI-first organizational transformation, global team architecture, and M&A integration within a PE-backed growth environment.

experience.title: VP, Software Engineering and Product Development
bullets: ## AI-First Organizational Transformation

Championed enterprise AI adoption against initial executive resistance—delivering a documented 3–4x increase in meaningful code output (500K → 1.5–2M lines) with 30%+ productivity improvement. Conceived and led the organization's first AI Hackathon, designed the AI Development Framework (Developer Directs → AI Executes → Developer Validates), and created the AI-Native PDLC—a 5-stage framework feeding curated context to both engineers and AI agents.

Built an AI Governance Architecture satisfying HIPAA-adjacent compliance: usage policies, security guardrails, cost governance, and audit protocols—converting ad-hoc experiments into governed, repeatable pipelines across the full SDLC.
## Global Capability Center & Team Scaling

Led the engineering implementation of a board-sponsored Global Capability Center—negotiating the delivery partner agreement, standing up wave-based hiring governance, and ensuring engineering readiness for a 60–65 person offshore organization.

Built and led a 57+ person multi-disciplinary engineering organization across multiple time zones (6 direct reports, 4+ teams, 20+ nearshore LATAM contractors) within a PE-backed SaaS scale-up—direct P&L accountability for $4.8M in strategic investments within a $23M R&D budget, delivering 35%+ efficiency improvement.
## M&A Integration & Operational Transformation

Led post-acquisition integration of the OnePacs engineering team—transforming small-shop operations into enterprise-grade delivery with CI/CD maturity, quality gates, FDA audit remediation, and cultural alignment under direct leadership.
## Platform Modernization & Engineering Reliability

Led engineering for a Healthcare SaaS platform serving 5,000+ urgent care providers; coached and guided the creation of the Architecture Hub (repo-based compliance scoring across security, reliability, maintainability, deployability, observability); drove AFC latency fix (6% → 0% failure rate); championed Jenkins → CircleCI migration completed in one day with zero incidents.
## Organizational Leadership & Strategic Operations

Designed quarterly leadership onsites with progressive maturity arc (Identity → Excellence → Focus → Growth → Recalibration → Sharpen the Standard). Served as technical ambassador to PE stakeholders. Championed people-first culture under financial constraint—advocating retention cases against committee pushback while maintaining top 10% performance outcomes.

experience.title: Progressive Leadership Across Regulated & Enterprise Environments
bullets: ## Customer Success & Enterprise Architecture

Chronicled (2022–2023): Led enterprise blockchain implementations for Fortune 100 pharmaceutical customers via GCP-hosted MediLedger; served as Head of Customer Success Services managing implementation consultants with deep Kubernetes configuration expertise.

SafeNet Consulting (2021–2022): Led architecture team delivering multi-tier, cloud-native solutions for Fortune 500 enterprise clients across AWS and Azure—identifying emerging technology opportunities and translating them into actionable adoption strategies.
SysLogic (2019–2021): Championed cloud-first modernization across multiple enterprise engagements—governing solution design, guiding infrastructure adoption, and mentoring engineering talent.
## Regulated Platform & Delivery Leadership

HSA Bank (2014–2019): Dual VP/Director roles over 5 years—architected enterprise integration patterns, governed technology adoption on a regulated financial services platform, reduced support rework by 43% YoY, partnered with CTO on technical strategy.
CoreLogic / MSB (2011–2014): Defined roadmaps and delivered enterprise solutions with full technical specifications, mentoring distributed remote and onsite teams.
Health Payment Systems (2010–2011): Managed software development team ensuring constant improvement, engagement, and productivity across delivery cycles.

items: OCI Certified — Cloud Infrastructure 2024 Generative AI Professional

aiPractitioner.title: AI-Augmented Engineering — Practitioner & Architect Record
aiPractitioner.intro: Built Experity's AI-augmented engineering practice from zero—personally evaluating tooling, defining governance, running the organization's first AI hackathon, and designing the AI-Native PDLC framework before scaling adoption across 57+ engineers. Every policy was grounded in practitioner experience, not delegated from a distance.
bullets: AI Transformation Leadership: Championed AI adoption against initial executive resistance—built the business case, ran Experity's first-ever AI Hackathon, and drove cultural shift from skepticism to embedded practice. Delivered documented 3–4x productivity increase (500K → 1.5–2M lines of meaningful code) with 30%+ cycle time improvement across engineering teams.

AI-Native PDLC Design: Created a 5-stage Product Development Lifecycle (Signal → Context → Decision → Execution → Memory) with an AI Workspace for Product—feeding curated context packets to both human engineers and AI coding agents. Invented the 'Cold-Read Validation' method proving artifact quality before build begins.

Governance Architecture: Designed the organization's AI adoption framework—LLM usage policies, no-auto-merge standards, code review quality gates for AI-generated output, per-team cost tracking, security guardrails, and HIPAA-adjacent audit protocols. Converted ad-hoc tool experiments into governed, repeatable, audited pipelines.

Operating Model Shift: Led transition from manual code producers to agent-augmented reviewers—establishing that the team's constraint shifted from build capacity to review quality (a fundamentally better bottleneck). Defined the Developer Directs → AI Executes → Developer Validates workflow with 80/20 architectural oversight model.

AI-Compressed Incident Response: Deployed AI-assisted runbooks and knowledge retrieval pipelines that compressed initial triage times, automated structured post-incident learning generation, and reduced on-call cognitive load across distributed teams.

Architecture Hub: Coached and guided architecture leadership toward building a repo-based compliance scoring platform—injecting the 'Well-Architected Framework' concept over time, shaping the vision and assessment model across security, reliability, maintainability, deployability, and observability pillars. Enabled automated architecture governance at scale.

Practitioner Certification: OCI Certified — Cloud Infrastructure 2024 Generative AI Professional—hands-on credential covering prompt engineering, RAG architectures, fine-tuning, and responsible GenAI system design. Used to inform architecture decisions and lead AI enablement conversations with credibility.

strategicInitiatives.name: Enterprise AI Adoption & Engineering Productivity Transformation
strategicInitiatives.summary: Championed enterprise-wide AI adoption against initial executive resistance—delivering a documented 3–4x increase in meaningful code output and 30%+ productivity improvement across a ~41-person engineering organization.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: Faced with an organizational mandate to avoid AI entirely, built the business case from scratch and drove a transformation that became the defining achievement of the engineering org. Designed the AI Development Framework, created the AI-Native PDLC, ran the organization's first-ever AI Hackathon, and established the governance architecture that made AI adoption safe, auditable, and HIPAA-adjacent compliant. The core insight that shaped everything: 'The team's constraint shifted from build capacity to review quality—a fundamentally better bottleneck.'
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: Conceived the vision, built the business case against institutional resistance, and designed the frameworks from scratch. The engineering teams executed within the model—Guillermo created the enabling conditions, governance, and cultural momentum that made execution possible.
frameworks: Developer Directs → AI Executes → Developer Validates
keyDetails: Starting point: executive mandate was to avoid AI entirely—organizational risk aversion was the initial blocker
Meaningful code output grew from ~500K (peak, pre-AI) to ~1.5–2M lines across architectural platform work, medical imaging rewrites, and document processing improvements
Embedded AI directly into customer-facing product offerings, not just internal engineering tooling
Surfaced structural gap: engineering velocity began outpacing product definition capacity—prompted redesign of the product-to-engineering handoff model
Applied 'definition-first, then iteration' discipline—evaluating cost-to-value ratios before recommending agentic approaches, avoiding trend-following
metrics: First-ever AI Hackathon conceived and led

strategicInitiatives.name: Global Engineering Capability Center — Implementation Leadership
strategicInitiatives.summary: Leading the engineering implementation of an executive-sponsored Global Capability Center in Bangalore—coordinating cross-functional workstreams, establishing governance cadence, and ensuring engineering readiness for a 60–65 person offshore organization.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: Assigned as the engineering leader responsible for operationalizing a board-level GCC decision. Negotiated the delivery partner agreement on engineering's behalf, then stood up the implementation structure: wave-based hiring coordination, weekly governance cadences across HR/IT/Finance/TA, and an AI-first readiness mandate for all incoming engineers. Built the employer value proposition and LinkedIn presence from scratch in a market where the organization had no brand recognition. The work demands experienced coordination across many stakeholders with competing priorities—applying operational discipline to a complex, multi-threaded initiative that engineering didn't originate but must make succeed.
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: This was not Guillermo's initiative—it was a board-level strategic decision. His role is operational leadership: negotiating the engineering agreement, standing up governance, coordinating cross-functional workstreams, and ensuring the engineering organization is prepared to integrate a 60–65 person offshore team successfully.
keyDetails: Board-level strategic decision—engineering's role is operationalizing the implementation, not originating the strategy
Negotiated the delivery partner agreement on engineering's behalf and transitioned immediately to structured implementation
Target: 60–65 engineers, QA, and delivery managers across structured wave-based hiring cohorts
Established weekly workstream cadence (TA, HR, IT, Branding) and monthly steering committee with executive sponsorship
AI-first readiness mandate: ensuring all GCC hires can operate within AI-augmented engineering workflows from day one
Built employer branding and LinkedIn presence from zero—competing for talent against hyperscalers without existing brand recognition

strategicInitiatives.name: Engineering Architecture Compliance Platform — Architecture Hub
strategicInitiatives.summary: Guided and enabled architecture leadership to build a proprietary repo-based Architecture Hub—assessing every service against a multi-pillar compliance framework for continuous, data-driven visibility into engineering health.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: Injected the 'Well-Architected Framework' concept over time and shaped the vision and assessment model until the team had clarity to build. The Hub scores every service across five pillars (Security, Reliability, Maintainability, Deployability, Observability), surfaces dependency mapping, and shifts governance from reactive review to proactive data-driven decisions. Also sponsored evaluation of a cloud-native application protection platform (CNAPP) to strengthen the security pillar—assessing runtime exploitability, API endpoint discovery, and code-to-cloud PR commenting.
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: Provided the conceptual framework, validated the approach, removed organizational barriers, and enabled the architecture team to build and own the platform. The Hub was designed by Guillermo's vision; built and operated by the architecture team.
keyDetails: 5 compliance pillars: Security, Reliability, Maintainability, Deployability, Observability—scored per service with visual dependency mapping
Shift-left security: evaluated and piloted a CNAPP (cloud-native application protection platform) assessing runtime exploitability and API endpoint discovery
Code-to-cloud PR commenting capability evaluated: deploy / caution / block recommendations surfaced at PR time
Vendor evaluation assessed complementarity with existing endpoint protection tools rather than replacement
Roadmap includes attack surface management and deeper CI/CD integration for build-time risk gating
metrics: 5-pillar assessment model across full service portfolio

strategicInitiatives.name: Integration Platform Transformation — Manual to Self-Service
strategicInitiatives.summary: Identified and drove a strategic restructuring of the integration function—consolidating a 17,000+ pipeline operations team under platform engineering to eliminate overhead, close skills gaps, and shift from maintenance to transformation.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: Identified a structural misalignment: the integration team maintained 17,000+ active pipelines with strong operational reliability but no strategic growth trajectory, and communication overhead between integration and platform development was creating organizational drag. Applied a waste-elimination methodology to surface transformation opportunities and designed a consolidation that repositioned the team as future integration leaders—not a maintenance function. Framed the change as 'evolution, not disruption,' preserving tribal knowledge while opening the path to API and MCP integration capabilities.
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: Identified the structural gap, designed the consolidation rationale, framed the change management approach, and directed the restructuring. The team executes the new model; Guillermo designed the organizational architecture and the narrative that made change possible.
keyDetails: Scale: 17,000+ active integration pipelines with strong reliability track record but no strategic growth path
Consolidation eliminated communication hops between integration and platform development, reducing decision latency
Skills gap identified: missing solution architect expertise, API integration capabilities, and customer-facing decision-tree mindset
Change management framing: 'evolution, not disruption'—emphasizing tribal knowledge value and positioning the team as future integration leaders
MCP (Model Context Protocol) integration capabilities identified as a key growth area for the consolidated team
GCC engineering resources positioned to supplement API/MCP capability gaps within 6-month transformation window

strategicInitiatives.name: AI-Native Product Development Lifecycle
strategicInitiatives.summary: Created a 5-stage PDLC (Signal → Context → Decision → Execution → Memory) redesigning how Product and Engineering collaborate when AI agents make build cycles cheaper than product definition.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: Surfaced and addressed a structural insight that changed how the organization thinks about product-to-engineering handoff: when AI makes implementation cheap, the bottleneck shifts to product clarity. Designed a complete Product Development Lifecycle treating AI agents as first-class participants—feeding curated context packets to both engineers and AI coding agents. Facilitated a 2-day structured onsite to prove the model, inventing the 'Cold-Read Validation' method to prove artifact quality before build begins.
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: Conceived the framework, designed the 5-stage pipeline model, facilitated the proving onsite, and invented the Cold-Read Validation method. The product and engineering teams operate within the framework; Guillermo designed the architecture and drove adoption.
keyDetails: Core insight: 'Implementation has gotten cheap. The product process is not as compressible.'—reframing the organization's true constraint
5-stage pipeline defines how product documentation becomes AI agent context, not just human specification
Cold-Read Validation method: prove artifact quality before build begins, independent of the author
78% Day 1 readiness score achieved in the structured proving onsite—validated the framework's practicality
Surfaces organizational need to redesign the product-to-engineering handoff at a structural level

strategicInitiatives.name: Asynchronous Messaging Platform — Performance Remediation
strategicInitiatives.summary: Led the diagnosis and resolution of a critical performance degradation in a high-volume async messaging platform—reducing clinical workflow message latency failures from 6% to 0% and structured an executive communication campaign framing the outcome as a model for engineering problem-solving.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: A clinical workflow messaging platform was delivering 6% of messages outside the acceptable latency window—a reliability gap with direct patient care implications. Led the diagnostic framing and directed the resolution strategy (channel separation + polling configuration), achieving 0% failure rate within 24 hours of the root cause fix. Personally designed the structured communication narrative for the CTO organization and product stakeholders—framing the fix as a teaching moment on proactive engineering, not just incident closure. Executed while simultaneously managing org restructuring, GCC implementation, and a team role-fit assessment.
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: Directed the diagnostic approach, oversaw the remediation, and designed the stakeholder communication narrative. The engineering team implemented the channel separation and polling changes; Guillermo framed the problem, removed blockers, and structured the executive story.
keyDetails: Baseline: 6% of messages exceeded 2–3 minute delivery window in a clinical context where latency has direct patient care implications
Channel separation change immediately reduced failure rate to 2%; polling configuration update brought it to 0% within 24 hours
100% of messages delivered under one minute post-fix; two-thirds under one minute consistently
Communication strategy: designed structured narrative (problem → solution → results) broadcast to CTO organization and product teams
Framed the fix as a teaching moment on proactive engineering problem-solving—not just incident closure
Executed while simultaneously managing org restructuring, GCC implementation, and a team role-fit assessment
metrics: Executive communication campaign designed and deployed

strategicInitiatives.name: M&A Integration & Team Operationalization
strategicInitiatives.summary: Led post-acquisition integration of the OnePacs engineering team—transforming small-shop operations into enterprise-grade delivery with CI/CD maturity, quality gates, FDA audit remediation, and cultural alignment.
strategicInitiatives.narrative: Absorbed a small-shop imaging engineering team into the enterprise delivery organization following acquisition—establishing CI/CD maturity, quality gates, and compliance frameworks from near-zero. Directed FDA audit remediation and cultural alignment work that enabled the team to operate at enterprise standards while maintaining product continuity throughout the integration period. Built the organizational bridge between startup delivery norms and enterprise operating standards without losing the team's product knowledge or pace.
strategicInitiatives.roleDescription: Directed the integration strategy and held accountability for outcomes. Engineering leads executed the technical implementations; Guillermo set the standards, cleared organizational friction, and ensured cultural alignment across the combined team.
keyDetails: Team absorbed under direct leadership with full accountability for delivery outcomes and continuity
CI/CD pipelines, quality gates, and compliance frameworks established from near-zero—not inherited
FDA audit remediation completed—bringing the team to regulated delivery standards required for the platform
Product continuity maintained throughout the integration period despite significant operational change
Cultural alignment bridged small-shop engineering norms to enterprise operating standards—no talent loss
metrics: Team fully integrated under unified leadership

leadershipFrameworks.name: AI Development Framework
leadershipFrameworks.tagline: Developer Directs → AI Executes → Developer Validates
leadershipFrameworks.description: The operating model that shifted the engineering organization from manual code production to agent-augmented review. Engineers provide context, intent, and architectural direction; AI agents generate first-draft code, PR descriptions, test cases, and documentation; engineers validate, refine, and own the output. The bottleneck moves from build capacity to review quality—a fundamentally better constraint. Four core principles: Provide Blueprints, Iterate in Cycles, Delegate Systematics, Shift Your Skills.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Designed from practitioner experience—personally evaluated AI tooling before institutionalizing as governance policy. Documented as an interactive infographic with data visualizations showing the 80/20 oversight shift.

leadershipFrameworks.name: BMAD Method — AI-Driven Agile Development
leadershipFrameworks.tagline: Analyst → PM → Architect → Scrum Master → Developer → QA
leadershipFrameworks.description: An agentic software development lifecycle where specialized AI agents handle each phase—Analyst for ideation, PM for requirements, Architect for technical design, Scrum Master for story drafting, Developer for implementation, QA for compliance. Context engineering ensures each agent receives only the precise information needed. Document-driven: the process revolves around creating and refining artifacts that serve as context for each step.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Adapted and documented as an organizational framework—bridging the gap between ad-hoc AI tool usage and governed, repeatable agentic delivery pipelines.

leadershipFrameworks.name: A-D-V-R Cycle — AI Hackathon Workflow
leadershipFrameworks.description: A high-speed iterative workflow designed for the organization's first-ever AI Hackathon. Positions the developer as Architect and the AI as Apprentice. Each cycle: provide a precise small task from the blueprint, AI generates the output, developer validates and approves before the next cycle begins. Emphasizes that the developer's value shifts from typing speed to clarity of architectural thinking.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Created for and deployed during the first AI Hackathon—a cultural transformation event conceived and led to shift the organization from AI skepticism to embedded practice.

leadershipFrameworks.name: AI-Native Product Development Lifecycle
leadershipFrameworks.description: A 5-stage PDLC that treats AI agents as first-class participants alongside human engineers. Each stage produces curated context packets consumed by both humans and AI coding agents. Addresses the structural insight that AI has made implementation cheap—shifting the true organizational constraint to product definition quality. Includes the Cold-Read Validation method proving artifact quality before build begins.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Created and piloted through a structured 2-day onsite proving session with the product and engineering teams. 78% Day 1 readiness score validated the framework's practicality.

leadershipFrameworks.name: Architecture Assessment Model
leadershipFrameworks.description: Five pillars scored per service against defined standards. Powers continuous, automated visibility into engineering health across the full software estate. Shifts governance from reactive post-deployment review to proactive, data-driven risk surfacing before code reaches production. Includes dependency mapping, shift-left security integration, and self-service governance capabilities.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Injected as a concept over time into the architecture team; shaped the Architecture Hub's scoring methodology and dependency mapping approach.

leadershipFrameworks.name: Security Design & IP Protection Framework
leadershipFrameworks.tagline: Tiered Access · Context Boundaries · Audit Protocols
leadershipFrameworks.description: A structured approach to securing AI-augmented engineering workflows in regulated environments. Defines tiered contractor security models, establishes context boundaries for AI tooling (what data AI agents can and cannot access), and implements audit protocols satisfying HIPAA-adjacent compliance requirements. Ensures AI adoption does not compromise the organization's security posture or intellectual property.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Designed to address the organizational risk concerns that initially blocked AI adoption—turning security from a blocker into an enabler through governance architecture.

leadershipFrameworks.name: Leadership Maturation Arc
leadershipFrameworks.tagline: Identity → Excellence → Focus → Growth → Recalibration → Sharpen
leadershipFrameworks.description: A deliberate multi-quarter progression designed for an engineering leadership team. Each quarter's theme builds intentionally on the last—producing artifacts, operating models, and shared language that compound over time. The arc treats leadership development as a designed system, not a series of disconnected offsite events.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Designed and facilitated across six consecutive quarterly leadership onsites, each building on the artifacts of the last.

leadershipFrameworks.name: Agentic Architecture Discipline
leadershipFrameworks.description: Before recommending any agentic approach, evaluate the cost-to-value ratio against the specific use case. Avoid architectural trend-following. The principle: definition precedes iteration. Surfaces the insight that the real bottleneck in AI-augmented engineering is often product definition quality—not build capacity—and ensures AI investments deliver genuine leverage rather than novelty.
leadershipFrameworks.origin: Developed through practitioner evaluation of AI tooling patterns; applied across integration and product transformation initiatives.
